_position_sizing_note: available_capital_paper_mode is the simulated capital basis for paper mode's risk-cap checks (max_risk_per_trade_pct) -- no real broker balance is consulted in paper mode. Live mode always sources NLV/buying power from tastytrade directly via tt.py get_account_info.
_tastytrade_costs_note: Models tastytrade's real commission schedule (source: tastytrade.com/pricing, 'Commissions & Fees' doc last updated 2026-04-06) for paper-mode cost-adjusted P&L -- see src/costs.py. commission_close_per_contract is 0 by design (tastytrade's distinctive open-only model). clearing/regulatory rates are approximations of pass-through exchange/ORF/FINRA-TAF/SEC fees and change periodically -- re-check the source doc periodically.
_profiles_note: Named risk profiles for paper-mode testing (see docs/paper-trading-profiles.md). Each layers over base config: strategy_defaults -> per-strategy -> profile overrides. risk_pct_multiplier scales every strategy's max_risk_per_trade_pct; tier_floor is 'Tier 1' (only) or 'Tier 2' (Tier 1+2). Selected via --profile; omitting it uses base config unchanged (profile 'default').

aggressive.description: Larger sizing and more concurrent positions, Tier 1+2.

iron_fly._wing_width_note: wing_width_credit_multiple is the fallback if iv_rv_ratio can't be refetched at entry time; otherwise superseded by the bands below, scaled to this candidate's own IV/RV ratio (not market-wide VIX -- an earnings move is idiosyncratic to one stock, so its own IV/RV is a more relevant regime signal here than broad market volatility). Reasoned proposal, not backtested for this strategy.
iron_fly._exit_note: Step 3c (market-open-to-close-window) profit-target/stop-loss check via scanner.evaluate_credit_spread_exit, ahead of Step 3's unconditional close-window sweep. Calibrated against earnings-specific short-straddle/iron-butterfly research (not the generic weeks-long income-strategy conventions, which don't transfer to a ~16-hour overnight-gap trade): 50% of max profit as a target, stop at 1.5x credit received. Not backtested for this project specifically.
iron_fly._liquidity_note: Shared liquidity gates (scanner.py's apply_liquidity_gates) -- reasoned starting points, not backtested for this strategy. max_bid_ask_spread_pct at 15% of mid is generous enough not to reject normal earnings-week widening but catches genuinely illiquid chains. min_market_cap at $2B (mid-cap floor) favors names liquid enough that options market-makers stay engaged around an earnings event. min_combined_option_volume (front-month chain-wide daily contract volume, same scope as min_combined_open_interest) is a conservative starting floor.

double_calendar._note: Stricter liquidity/expected-move floors than iron_fly -- execution cost matters more across two expirations, and a single tail-risk surprise hurts a debit trade more than iron_fly's width-defined max loss. Reasoned starting points, not backtested for this strategy.
double_calendar._exit_note: Calibrated against published backtests/practitioner guidance, not backtested for this strategy specifically: profit target of 20-30% of max profit shows improved profitability and less time-at-risk in structured studies (25% used as the midpoint); 5-7 DTE front-month exit is described as non-negotiable since gamma risk on a short ATM option overwhelms theta benefit inside that window (5 used, not the looser 2-3 day figure); stop_loss_pct_of_debit is a whole-position backstop for when the spread's value round-trips back toward the entry debit, separate from leg_stop_delta_abs which handles a single threatened side.

expected_move_butterfly._note: 1 long ATM + 2 short at the expected-move strike + 1 long further OTM, equidistant (ATM-to-short == short-to-far). Side (call or put) picked via a 25-delta risk reversal (skew_delta_target) -- the industry-standard delta-symmetric skew measure, NOT compared at the expected-move strikes themselves (which aren't delta-symmetric once skew is present, and would conflate structural single-name put skew -- present on most names regardless of any earnings catalyst -- with a real earnings-specific signal). min_skew_abs is a reasoned starting point -- small enough not to reject every candidate, large enough to filter pure noise -- but does not fully correct for structural skew, which would need a per-name pre-earnings skew baseline this project doesn't have data for. Not backtested for this strategy.
expected_move_butterfly._exit_note: Step 3c (market-open-to-close-window) profit-target/stop-loss check via scanner.evaluate_debit_spread_exit, ahead of Step 3's unconditional close-window sweep. Calibrated against debit-butterfly-specific research: 25% of max profit as a target, a stop at a 40% loss of the debit paid -- deliberately tighter than double_calendar's calendar-spread-calibrated stop_loss_pct_of_debit (1.0), since this is a structurally different, faster-resolving overnight position. Not backtested for this project specifically.

iron_condor._note: Same credit-spread-plus-wings shape as iron_fly, but short strikes sit at the expected-move boundary (1.0x, same convention as double_calendar/expected_move_butterfly) instead of ATM -- backtests show a wider profit zone, lower credit, and a materially different historical win rate than iron fly. Not backtested for this project's specific implementation.
iron_condor._exit_note: Step 3c (market-open-to-close-window) profit-target/stop-loss check via scanner.evaluate_credit_spread_exit, same convention and rationale as iron_fly's -- 50% of max profit target, 1.5x credit stop. Not backtested for this project specifically.

atm_calendar._note: Sell a front-month ATM call, buy the same strike in a later monthly expiration -- same front/back term-structure edge as double_calendar, one option type instead of two, always the call side (config-fixed, not skew-selected -- call/put ATM calendars perform virtually identically). Always closes as a single 2-leg unit (legs_json only, never trade_legs), unlike double_calendar's independently-closeable sides. No expected-move-boundary filter -- this is a pure ATM term-structure play, not targeting where the stock lands. Reasoned starting points, not backtested for this strategy.
atm_calendar._exit_note: Same researched-convention shape as double_calendar's: profit target at 25-30% of max profit (midpoint of the 20-75% range described in practitioner guidance), stop when the debit has roughly doubled (stop_loss_pct_of_debit=1.0, matching evaluate_debit_spread_exit's convention -- note this is looser than expected_move_butterfly's 0.40 since a calendar's decay profile is slower-resolving), 5-day time-exit before front expiration for the same gamma-risk-overwhelms-theta reasoning as double_calendar's. Not backtested for this project specifically.

directional_credit_spread._note: Single-sided vertical credit spread (put spread if bullish, call spread if bearish), sold on whichever side the 25-delta risk reversal shows richer (expected_move_butterfly.select_side(), reused as-is -- min_skew_abs/skew_delta_target share that module's meaning). Genuinely new strike-selection convention for this project: the short strike is chosen so BREAKEVEN (short strike minus/plus credit received) lands at the expected-move boundary, not the strike itself -- see directional_credit_spread.py's _select_short_strike. Same overnight-gap hold model and Step 3/3c close mechanism as iron_fly/iron_condor. Reasoned starting points, not backtested for this strategy.
directional_credit_spread._exit_note: Step 3c (market-open-to-close-window) profit-target/stop-loss check via scanner.evaluate_credit_spread_exit, same convention and rationale as iron_fly's/iron_condor's -- 50% of max profit target, 1.5x credit stop. Not backtested for this project specifically.

broken_wing_butterfly._note: Body-anchored (no ATM leg): 2 short contracts at the expected-move strike (the body, side picked via a 25-delta risk reversal), protected by a narrow long wing toward current price and a wide long wing away from it, both sized directly off the body strike -- NOT expected_move_butterfly's 1-2-1 ATM/short/far shape. Near wing width scales per-candidate off the body leg's own premium via the IV/RV-banded wing_width_multiple_low/_mid/_high bands (deliberately smaller than iron_fly's 2.5/3.0/3.5, since this sizes a single-leg premium, not a straddle credit); far wing is wide_wing_multiple times the near wing. Reasoned starting point, not backtested for this strategy.
broken_wing_butterfly._credit_only_note: get_order hard-rejects (net_debit_positive_credit_required) any candidate that doesn't price out to a net credit or breakeven -- if a candidate's real premiums land as a net debit at these wing widths, they didn't buy back enough premium to finance the position, so it's rejected outright rather than entered as a smaller-debit butterfly. Raise wide_wing_multiple or the wing_width_multiple_* bands if too many real candidates are being rejected this way.
broken_wing_butterfly._exit_note: Step 3c (market-open-to-close-window) profit-target/stop-loss check via scanner.evaluate_debit_spread_exit, same convention and values as expected_move_butterfly's -- 25% of max profit target, a stop at a 40% loss of the debit paid. Not backtested for this project specifically.
